questions.question: What is Web Performance Fundamentals and why is it important?
questions.answer: Web Performance Fundamentals is a fundamental concept that Why performance matters, Core Web Vitals, and measuring speed. It's important because it forms the foundation for building robust and efficient applications. Understanding Web Performance Fundamentals allows developers to make better architectural decisions and write cleaner code.

questions.question: How do you apply Web Performance Fundamentals in a real project?
questions.answer: In practice, Web Performance Fundamentals is applied by following established patterns and best practices. Start with the simplest implementation, test thoroughly, and iterate. The key is to understand the underlying principles so you can adapt to different scenarios.

questions.question: What are common mistakes when working with Web Performance Fundamentals?
questions.answer: Common mistakes include: not understanding the fundamentals before applying advanced patterns, ignoring edge cases, not testing adequately, and over-engineering solutions. The best approach is to start simple and add complexity only when needed.

questions.question: How would you explain Web Performance Fundamentals to a beginner?
questions.answer: Web Performance Fundamentals is essentially about why performance matters, core web vitals, and measuring speed. Think of it as a building block — once you understand this concept, you can combine it with other concepts to build complex applications.
